object.setup_prose: As an academic affairs officer, you are reviewing two different datasets regarding undergraduate satisfaction levels. Dataset A surveys the incoming class of 2024 about their expectations in September, then surveys that exact same group again in May. Dataset B surveys a random sample of current students across all four years during the final week of the spring semester.
object.prompt: Based on the study designs described, which of the following is the most accurate characterization of these datasets?

options.text: Dataset A provides a snapshot of the entire undergraduate experience, while Dataset B tracks the individual development of the class of 2024.
options.feedback: Actually, you have reversed the labels. Dataset A is the longitudinal one because it follows a specific group over time, while Dataset B provides a single-moment snapshot.

options.text: Dataset A tracks changes within a specific group over time, while Dataset B provides a cross-sectional snapshot of the student body at one point in time.
options.feedback: Correct. Dataset A allows you to observe change within a cohort, while Dataset B allows you to observe differences across the current student body at a specific moment.

options.text: Both datasets are essentially the same because they provide an average satisfaction score for the university.
options.feedback: While both provide averages, they measure different things. Dataset A measures how individual sentiments evolve, whereas Dataset B provides a point-in-time view of current campus climate.

options.text: Dataset B is more reliable because it tracks the entire student population simultaneously, eliminating the need for cohort tracking.
options.feedback: Reliability depends on the goal. Dataset B is efficient for a current snapshot, but it cannot explain why individual students changed their perspectives over time the way a cohort study can.

object.rubric_primary: The learner correctly differentiates between a longitudinal cohort study and a cross-sectional snapshot, understanding the causal implications of tracking the same subjects over time.

visible_thinking_prompts.notice: What differences do you see in how these groups were selected and when they were surveyed?
visible_thinking_prompts.think: What kind of changes or trends can be captured in Dataset A versus Dataset B?
visible_thinking_prompts.wonder: Why would an administrator choose one method over the other for a university policy report?
